title: lepidium ginninderrense
intro: Lepidium ginninderrense, also known as Ginninderie pepperwort, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to Western Australia and has been used by the local Aboriginal people for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. It can be prepared as a herbal tea or used in salads, and has been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, fever, and sore throats.
